title: Protecting our Land Act
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.
plain_summary: This bill says the President must make new rules to stop certain foreign countries from buying land or buildings in America. The rules would block countries that are enemies of the United States or countries that help terrorists from purchasing any real estate. The bill covers all states and territories in America. The President would tell government agencies to create and enforce these new rules to protect American land from being bought by unfriendly foreign governments.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.
plain_keywords: ban foreign enemies from buying property
protect US property from foreign threats

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Protecting our Land Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Prohibition on Purchase of Public or Private Real Estate Located in the United States by Foreign Adversaries and State Sponsors of Terrorism
sections.text: The President must order all federal government departments and agencies to create rules that stop certain foreign groups from buying land or buildings in the United States. These rules must prevent purchases by foreign adversaries, countries that sponsor terrorism, anyone who works for these groups, and anyone owned or controlled by these groups. A foreign adversary means any foreign government or foreign person who regularly acts against America's safety or the safety of American people. A state sponsor of terrorism means a country whose government the Secretary of State has determined repeatedly supports international terrorism under specific laws including the Export Control Reform Act, the Foreign Assistance Act, the Arms Export Control Act, or other laws. The United States includes all fifty states, Washington DC, Puerto Rico, the Northern Mariana Islands, American Samoa, Guam, the US Virgin Islands, and any other US territory.
